title: A Tale of Two Cities
learningGoal: The learner will parse a 350-word comparative text utilizing dense adjective pairings (opposites), actively identifying both the 'Adjective + Noun' syntax and the 'Noun + Copula + Adjective' syntax.
instructions: Read the story comparing two very different cities. Notice how the writer describes things using adjectives. Pay close attention to how 'big buildings' never becomes 'bigs buildings'. Adjectives never take an 's'! Answer the 8 questions below.

contentItems.text: Notice there are two ways to describe something. You can say 'A big city' OR 'The city is big'. Both are perfect English.

readingText: I travel a lot. I have two favorite cities: New York and Kyoto. They are very different. New York is a big city. It is very loud. There are tall buildings everywhere. The streets are busy, and the cars are fast. I see many fast cars in New York. The food is good, but it is expensive. The people are fast, too. They always run. I like New York because it is an exciting place. Kyoto is a small city in Japan. It is very quiet. Kyoto is not loud. There are no tall buildings. There are old temples. The old temples are very beautiful. The streets are clean and slow. I see small cars and many bicycles. The food in Kyoto is hot and cheap. The people are very polite. They walk slowly. I like Kyoto because it is a peaceful place. Both cities have weather that changes. In summer, New York is hot. In winter, New York is very cold. It has white snow. Kyoto is also hot in summer, but the winter is mild. It is not very cold. Sometimes, it rains in Kyoto. The rain is cold. I have good friends in both cities. My friend in New York is a tall man. He is a loud man. He drives a fast car. My friend in Kyoto is a short woman. She is a quiet woman. She rides an old bicycle. They are different people, but they are both happy people. I am a happy traveler.
textInTargetLanguage: I travel a lot. I have two favorite cities: New York and Kyoto. They are very different. New York is a big city. It is very loud. There are tall buildings everywhere. The streets are busy, and the cars are fast. I see many fast cars in New York. The food is good, but it is expensive. The people are fast, too. They always run. I like New York because it is an exciting place. Kyoto is a small city in Japan. It is very quiet. Kyoto is not loud. There are no tall buildings. There are old temples. The old temples are very beautiful. The streets are clean and slow. I see small cars and many bicycles. The food in Kyoto is hot and cheap. The people are very polite. They walk slowly. I like Kyoto because it is a peaceful place. Both cities have weather that changes. In summer, New York is hot. In winter, New York is very cold. It has white snow. Kyoto is also hot in summer, but the winter is mild. It is not very cold. Sometimes, it rains in Kyoto. The rain is cold. I have good friends in both cities. My friend in New York is a tall man. He is a loud man. He drives a fast car. My friend in Kyoto is a short woman. She is a quiet woman. She rides an old bicycle. They are different people, but they are both happy people. I am a happy traveler.
baseLanguageSupportText: मैं बहुत यात्रा करता हूँ। मेरे दो पसंदीदा शहर हैं: न्यूयॉर्क और क्योटो। वे बहुत अलग हैं। न्यूयॉर्क एक बड़ा शहर है। यह बहुत शोरगुल वाला है। हर जगह ऊँची इमारतें हैं। सड़कें व्यस्त हैं, और कारें तेज़ हैं। मैं न्यूयॉर्क में कई तेज़ कारें देखता हूँ। भोजन अच्छा है, लेकिन यह महंगा है। लोग भी तेज़ हैं। वे हमेशा दौड़ते हैं। मुझे न्यूयॉर्क पसंद है क्योंकि यह एक रोमांचक जगह है। क्योटो जापान का एक छोटा शहर है। यह बहुत शांत है। क्योटो शोरगुल वाला नहीं है। वहाँ कोई ऊँची इमारतें नहीं हैं। वहाँ पुराने मंदिर हैं। पुराने मंदिर बहुत सुंदर हैं। सड़कें साफ और धीमी हैं। मैं छोटी कारें और कई साइकिलें देखता हूँ। क्योटो में भोजन गर्म और सस्ता है। लोग बहुत विनम्र हैं। वे धीरे चलते हैं। मुझे क्योटो पसंद है क्योंकि यह एक शांतिपूर्ण जगह है। दोनों शहरों का मौसम बदलता रहता है। गर्मियों में, न्यूयॉर्क गर्म होता है। सर्दियों में, न्यूयॉर्क बहुत ठंडा होता है। इसमें सफेद बर्फ होती है। क्योटो भी गर्मियों में गर्म होता है, लेकिन सर्दी हल्की होती है। यह बहुत ठंडा नहीं होता है। कभी-कभी, क्योटो में बारिश होती है। बारिश ठंडी होती है। दोनों शहरों में मेरे अच्छे दोस्त हैं। न्यूयॉर्क में मेरा दोस्त एक लंबा आदमी है। वह एक शोरगुल वाला आदमी है। वह एक तेज़ कार चलाता है। क्योटो में मेरी दोस्त एक छोटी (कद में) महिला है। वह एक शांत महिला है। वह एक पुरानी साइकिल चलाती है। वे अलग-अलग लोग हैं, लेकिन वे दोनों खुश लोग हैं। मैं एक खुश यात्री हूँ।

exampleSentence.targetText: New York is a big city.

exampleSentence.baseTranslation: वह एक शोर करने वाला आदमी है।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: New York is a big city.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: न्यूयॉर्क एक बड़ा शहर है। (Adjective comes BEFORE the noun 'city')

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: The old temples are very beautiful.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: द ओल्ड टेम्पल्स आर वेरी ब्यूटीफुल।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: पुराने मंदिर बहुत सुंदर हैं। (Old pluralizes with temples, but 'Old' stays singular without an 's')

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: I see many fast cars in New York.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: आई सी मेनी फास्ट कार्स इन न्यूयॉर्क।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: मैं न्यूयॉर्क में कई तेज़ कारें देखता हूँ। ('Fast cars' not 'Fasts cars')

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: In winter, New York is very cold.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: इन विंटर, न्यूयॉर्क इज़ वेरी कोल्ड।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: सर्दियों में, न्यूयॉर्क बहुत ठंडा होता है। (Adjective comes AFTER the 'is' verb)

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Look at the text: Does the writer say 'The talls buildings' or 'The tall buildings'?
What word is used to describe the food in New York?
True or False: The text says 'I have good friends in both cities.' The word 'good' does not have an 's', even though friends is plural.
What kind of car does the friend in New York drive? (Fill in the adjective: A _____ car).
What adjective is used to describe both friends at the very end of the story?
Is the woman in Kyoto described as 'tall' or 'short'?

expectedSummaryPoints: The learner recognizes the syntax of Adjective + Noun.
The learner inherently realizes adjectives never take plural forms in English sentences.
The learner correlates opposite adjectives (tall/short, big/small, loud/quiet).

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You perfectly processed over a dozen adjective pairings and correctly identified that modifying words NEVER take the letter 'S', no matter what.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Remember, in English, the describing word comes FIRST. 'Fast car', not 'Car fast'. Also, never say 'Talls buildings'!
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Check your answers about the food in New York. The text says it is 'good, but it is expensive'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: I travel a lot. I have two favorite cities... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Read the sentences out loud. Hearing 'Olds temples' sounds ridiculous to an English speaker. 'Old temples' sounds perfectly smooth.
commonMistakes: Thinking New York has hot food and cheap food.
